creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
Looks like syslog-ng doesn't use cap_enter(), so capabilities is not an option. Sorry for suggesting it.

For ACLs you'll need to read the setfacl(1) man page. If you use UFS you need to use tunefs(8) to enable either POSIX or NFSV4 ACLs. ZFS only supports NFSV4.

You could use ipfw, pf, or ipfilter to redirect ports.

I can't help you with ipfw redirects (last time I used ipfw was 20 years ago -- been using ipfilter since then -- not suggesting you switch either, use what you're comfortable with). For ipfilter I use the following to redirect port 80 to my squid port on my firewall/gateway.

rdr nfe0  0/0 port 80 -> 127.0.0.1  port 3129 tcp

For ipfw this (https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/redirect-udp-traffic-based-on-port-for-any-destination-address.60221/) is a good discussion. Note: the warning at the bottom applies to all firewalls. TCP segment offloading to the NIC firmware messes with the firewall resulting in packets being blocked when they should not be.

I'll ask our upline, the folks who wrote and maintain syslog-ng if they can provide assistance.
